  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Turn-of-nut match marks are absent or have not been rotated",
    "Inability to verify that the required pretension has been achieved in the structural bolting assembly"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the current bolting installation",
    "Instruct the ironworkers to properly match-mark and apply the required rotation per RCSC specifications, or verify tension using an approved alternative method",
    "Require a re-inspection by the third-party inspector once corrective action is complete"
  ],
  "rationale": "Under the RCSC Specification, when the turn-of-nut pretensioning method is specified, match-marking is a critical quality control step to visually verify that the nut has been rotated the required amount relative to the bolt shank. The absence of these marks or lack of rotation indicates the bolts are not properly pretensioned, which is a major structural non-compliance.",
  "references": [
    "RCSC Specification for Structural Joints Using High-Strength Bolts, Section 8.2.1 (Turn-of-Nut Pretensioning)"
  ]
}
